name: nursery
summary: A nursery — a cradle or small bed, a rocking chair for the nurse, toys scattered, a night-light burning low. Tender, cluttered, often uneasy.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: small rectangle or square, often adjoining a parent's chamber

primary_palette.note: infant nursery — cradle primary, wet-nurse arrangement, lullaby space
toddler nursery — a small bed, toys dominant, a child's kingdom
siblings' nursery — two small beds, shared toy pile, a governess's chair
mixed ages — infant and older sibling share the room, one asleep, one playing
playroom-style nursery — less sleeping, more playing, toys are primary

story_prompts: the baby sleeps — the nurse dozes in the rocker, a single candle burns blue
the children have been told to stay in their room — toys everywhere, nobody visible
the nursery is empty and has been for decades — dust on the rocking horse, the doll's eyes closed
a kidnapping has occurred — the cradle empty, the window open, a single small footprint
a ghost child plays here at night — toys in the morning are never where they were left
noble nursery with a hidden governess-room adjoining — a second door only servants know

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: soft low (0.25-0.4) — a night-light, a banked hearth, gentle and warm

anti_patterns: not a miniature adult bedroom — toys and playful clutter are the texture
avoid anything sharp or dangerous on display — nurseries are curated for safety
scale the props — a rocking chair for an adult, tiny chair/table for children
the feeling is TENDER or UNSETTLING depending on story — lean into whichever is chosen
don't crowd — leave play space, often a central rug with nothing on it
